title: Slow-cooker chicken curry
subtitle: A mild, everyday British curry with boneless thighs
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs slow-cooked in a spiced tomato and onion sauce with curry powder, garam masala, and ginger for six hours. A low-effort weeknight curry that is milder than a takeaway and completely adaptable. Add chilli to taste.
sourceNotes: The 'British chicken curry' is a distinct category from any specific regional Indian dish. It emerged from home cooking adaptations of restaurant curries from the 1970s onwards, using widely available curry powder blends rather than individually sourced spices. It is a mild, accessible dish that became a staple of British family cooking. The slow cooker version is the most common method for making it on a weeknight.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well for up to 3 months.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe and freeze in portions.

content.text: Fry the onion and spices before adding to the slow cooker. A raw onion dropped into the pot produces a different texture from a softened one. Five minutes in a pan with the curry powder blooms the spices and ensures the onion breaks down fully into the sauce rather than remaining in distinct pieces.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a pan over medium heat. Fry the {{onion}} for 5 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{curry-powder}}, {{turmeric}}, and {{tomato-puree}} and stir for 1 minute. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{stock-chicken}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Stir to combine. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the chicken is tender. Stir in the {{garam-masala}}, taste, and adjust seasoning.

items.cause: Curry powder not bloomed or brand is old
items.fix: Fry the curry powder in oil before adding it. Replace curry powder that is more than a year old — it loses potency quickly

items.cause: Medium or hot curry powder rather than mild
items.fix: Use mild curry powder and add a tablespoon of double cream or yoghurt to the finished dish to calm the heat

items.cause: Too much stock, or tinned tomatoes were very thin
items.fix: Reduce the stock to 100 ml. Remove the lid for the last 20 minutes on high to thicken

content.text: This is the weeknight family curry that most British households know. The slow cooker version requires 15 minutes in the morning and no further attention. Serve with basmati rice, naan, and mango chutney. Leftovers are excellent the next day with the sauce even thicker and spicier than when first served.
